_comment: Real-shape hook fixture corpus for tests/run_fixture_corpus.py (#117). Each case runs one hook under home/.claude/hooks/ against a committed fixture in the REAL Claude Code envelope shape and asserts its exit code (2 = block, 0 = allow). 'stdin' cases pipe a PreToolUse hook-input payload (pretooluse/*.json) straight to the hook. 'transcript' cases point the Stop hook at a transcript JSONL (transcripts/*.jsonl) via a synthesized Stop envelope. See README.md for how to regenerate/redact fixtures.

cases.desc: interpreter inline-code egress one-liner is blocked
gh api write in trailing argv position is blocked
a benign command is allowed
a gh api read (GET) is allowed
gh api graphql --input FILE is blocked — body unreadable, fails closed (#265)
block-egress ignores a non-Bash tool_name (real envelope)
blocks checkout of a branch held by another worktree, in the real envelope shape (#170)
allows checkout of the current worktree's own branch, in the real envelope shape (#170)
block-checkout-held-branch ignores a non-Bash tool_name (real envelope) (#170)
blocks a force-push that would discard remote commits not present locally, in the real envelope shape (#291)
allows a force-push to a brand-new branch with no remote-tracking ref yet, in the real envelope shape (#291)
blocks a forced clean that a dry run shows would actually remove files, in the real envelope shape (#291)
allows a dry-run clean (-n) even with untracked files present, in the real envelope shape (#291)
blocks rm -rf {realdir1,realdir2} — brace expansion resolves to two real non-empty dirs, never one nonexistent literal (#365)
allows rm -rf {emptydir1,emptydir2} — every brace-expanded path is provably safe, expansion classifies rather than blanket-blocks (#365)
blocks a sleep-based polling loop, in the real envelope shape (#191)
allows a bare rate-limiting sleep with no loop, in the real envelope shape (#191)
blocks sleep as the loop's own condition (while sleep N; do ...), in the real envelope shape (#229)
blocks stderr redirected to /dev/null, in the real envelope shape (#191)
allows stderr duplicated onto stdout, not discarded, in the real envelope shape (#191)
blocks the >/dev/null 2>&1 idiom, in the real envelope shape (#201)
blocks the 2>&- fd-close idiom, in the real envelope shape (#205)
dispatcher (#163) rail UNREGISTERED (Colin 2026-07-22): real envelope passes through despite matching block-egress.py  (#167)
dispatcher (#163) allows a benign command through its own envelope parsing (#167)
dispatcher (#163) routes a real envelope into block-checkout-held-branch.py and blocks it (#170)
dispatcher (#163) allows the current branch's own checkout via the checkout rail, real envelope (#170)
dispatcher (#163) routes a real envelope into block-sleep-loop.py and blocks it (#191)
dispatcher (#163) rail UNREGISTERED (Colin 2026-07-22): real envelope passes through despite matching block-suppressed-stderr.py  (#191)
generic subagent_type=claude with no model= is blocked
the same delegation under the historical tool_name 'Task' is also blocked — the gate must fire on either name (#138)
subagent_type=fork is exempt
a named subagent_type (Explore) is exempt
an explicit model= is allowed
require-delegation ignores a non-Agent tool_name (real envelope)
claim over edited .py with no verification is blocked; also pins #80 (tool-result records skipped when finding the turn boundary)
a real pytest tool_use this turn counts as evidence — allowed
a claim over a docs-only (README.md) edit does not fire
a bare /verify MENTION in assistant prose (no actual tool call) is not evidence — blocked (#83)
a bare /bet MENTION in assistant prose (no actual tool call) is not evidence — blocked (#83)
a bare /run MENTION in assistant prose (no actual tool call) is not evidence — blocked (#83)
a standalone /verify invocation turn edits no product code — allowed (see #109 in README for the tag-shape caveat)
a fast-forward commit since the previous PostToolUse snapshot is not flagged, in the real envelope shape (#344)
a hard reset that discards a commit unreachable from any other ref is flagged, in the real envelope shape (#344)
